brief:
**에너지 CVC 투자 브리핑 (2026.03.28)**

AI 데이터센터 전력 수요 급증이 에너지 저장 시장의 새로운 성장 동력으로 부상하고 있다. Form Energy가 미국 AI 데이터센터 대상 12GWh 규모 철-공기 배터리 공급 계약을 체결한 것은 장기간 저장이 가능한 차세대 기술에 대한 실질적 수요 증명이다. 동시에 American Battery Factory의 애리조나 LFP 기가팩토리 4.5GWh 인수 계약과 영국 AR7 라운드 차액계약 체결 등 대규모 프로젝트들이 연이어 가시화되고 있어, 에너지 저장 밸류체인 전반의 투자 기회가 확대되고 있다. Gore Street Capital이 BESS 사전 건설 단계 데이터 관리의 중요성을 강조한 점도 운영 효율성에 집중하는 시장 성숙도를 보여준다.

signals.title: Form Energy signs 12GWh agreement to supply multi-day iron-air batteries to new US AI data centres
signals.summary: Form Energy has signed a 12GWh supply agreement in the US for its iron-air batteries with AI data centre infrastructure developer Crusoe.

signals.title: American Battery Factory secures 4.5GWh offtake agreements for Arizona LFP gigafactory
signals.summary: American Battery Factory (AFB) has secured offtake agreements for 4.5GWh of its initial 5.5GWh US factory output.

signals.title: UK’s LCCC Signs Contracts for Difference with AR7 Winners
signals.summary: The UK Low Carbon Contracts Company (LCCC) has signed Contracts for Difference (CfD) with […]

signals.title: The Hydrogen Stream: Capacity outlook grows, deals shift
signals.summary: GlobalData says hydrogen capacity could reach 82.3 million metric tons per annum (mtpa) by 2030, while Topsoe has ended supply agreements with First Ammonia.

signals.title: ‘Do not overlook it at pre-construction’: Gore Street Capital’s head of asset management on BESS data
signals.summary: BESS fund manager Gore Street Capital's director of asset management Daniel Sherlock-Burke recently discussed the work it is doing around capturing and making use of its huge quantities of operational data.

signals.title: ‘Be kind to yourself and patient with your journey’
signals.summary: This week Women in Solar+ Europe gives voice to Irene Mihai, Policy Director at the Romanian Photovoltaic Industry Association (RPIA). She says her personal experiences show how mentorship, collaboration, and inclusive workplace practices are essential for attracting and retaining talent while empowering the next generation to contribute to a sustainable future.

signals.title: Data center buildouts slowed late last year
signals.summary: “Power and electrical equipment is still the main driver of construction delays,” said Gordon Dolven, CBRE data center research director.

filteredOut.title: Fleet electrification is running into the grid. Planning and operations need to catch up.
filteredOut.summary: Thoughtfully planned, flexible load offers one answer to the grid’s present challenges, write experts from Synop and the Electric Power Research Institute.

filteredOut.title: PJM data center colocation plan takes fire from Vistra, data center group, others
filteredOut.summary: “Even a customer that brings sufficient co-located generation to meet its load cannot avoid curtailment risk,” the Data Center Coalition told the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission. “It is unclear why a customer would pursue this pathway at all.”

filteredOut.title: Winter Storm Fern highlighted need for expanded interregional transmission, Senate hears
filteredOut.summary: Some regions saw prices of hundreds of dollars per megawatt-hour, while neighboring areas experienced negative power prices. “There was power not being used at all because the transmission was not available,” said Liza Reed of the Niskanen Center think tank.

filteredOut.title: Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind begins delivering power
filteredOut.summary: CVOW is the largest offshore wind project in the U.S., with 2.6 GW of planned capacity. For now, a single, 14.7-MW turbine is generating power, and transmission upgrades in the PJM Interconnection are needed for full deliverability.

filteredOut.title: Southeast, lower-cost PJM states offer model for affordable grid expansion
filteredOut.summary: The vertically integrated utility model shows it is possible to build new generation and protect customers from steep rate increases, writes former FERC Commissioner Bernard McNamee.

filteredOut.title: 50 GW of data centers online at end of 2025, with MISO seeing strongest growth: FERC
filteredOut.summary: ERCOT, the Southwest Power Pool and the Southeast also saw rapid growth, but not as much as the 43% annual growth in the Midcontinent Independent System Operator region since 2020, FERC said.

filteredOut.title: DOE offers $50M for tribal energy projects
filteredOut.summary: The funding opportunity outlines program policy factors that can influence the selection of projects for awards, including “the degree to which the proposed project will support the supply of firm, reliable power.”

filteredOut.title: Michigan, New York lawmakers consider virtual power plant bills
filteredOut.summary: The proposals would prohibit utility ownership of participating distributed energy resources and require reasonable access for third-party aggregators.

filteredOut.title: Utility-scale solar and wind hit a record 17% of US generation in 2025: EIA
filteredOut.summary: “Combining utility-scale and small-scale solar generation in 2025 increases the share of wind and solar to 19% of total net generation,” said the Energy Information Administration.

filteredOut.title: China PV Industry Brief: China adds 32.48 GW solar as polysilicon prices fall
filteredOut.summary: China's National Energy Administration (NEA) says newly installed solar capacity reached 32.48 GW in the first two months of 2026, while the China Nonferrous Metals Industry Association (CNMIA) says polysilicon prices fell by as much as 7.95% in the latest week.

filteredOut.title: Agrivoltaics with trackers, vertical systems offer economic upside
filteredOut.summary: An industry analysis argues that agrivoltaic systems using trackers or vertical designs can outperform conventional solar on both revenues and land use, challenging conclusions from a recent German study.

filteredOut.title: Bluetti targets home backup with fridge-focused battery system
filteredOut.summary: Bluetti says its FridgePower system offers a plug-and-play battery solution designed to keep refrigerators and key household devices running during outages, without permanent installation.

filteredOut.title: European prosecutor investigates suspected solar tender fraud in Slovenia
filteredOut.summary: The European Public Prosecutor's Office is investigating an unnamed solar company after it breached conditions of a public tender for the co-financing of small solar plants. The office says house searches were carried out this week by Slovenian police, leading to the seizure of documents, electronic data and devices.

filteredOut.title: Brazil PV module imports fall 24% to 17.9 GW in 2025
filteredOut.summary: Greener says Brazil imported 17.9 GWp of PV modules in 2025, with distributed generation accounting for 79% of volumes despite a slowdown in installations.

filteredOut.title: US ITC opens Section 337 probe into global TOPCon supply chain
filteredOut.summary: The US International Trade Commission (ITC) has opened a Section 337 investigation into tunnel oxide passivated contact (TOPCon) solar cells, modules, panels, and related products following a complaint by First Solar, naming 47 entities across 11 countries as respondents.

filteredOut.title: Inland Rail deploys solar-powered level crossings in Australia
filteredOut.summary: Australia’s Inland Rail project says it has installed standalone solar power systems at two level crossings, removing the need for grid connections as construction progresses.

filteredOut.title: What are the selling points of marine energy?
filteredOut.summary: Marine energy  is renewable and generally predictable. It could thus compensate for the unplanned intermittency of solar and wind generation, its proponents argue. But several challenges still need to be overcome for the energy to become widely used.

filteredOut.title: UK’s first community-owned solar battery seeks investors
filteredOut.summary: Low Carbon Hub plans to add battery energy storage system (BESS) to existing solar plant. The developer is offering shares in what’s thought to be the first community-owned co-located battery in the United Kingdom.

filteredOut.title: Solar generation performance with regional weather conditions deviating from long-term averages
filteredOut.summary: Global solar PV continues its rapid growth, reaching around 650 GW in 2025, with record solar irradiation extremes across regions such as East Asia, India, and Latin America. With current production capacity and emerging technologies like perovskite-silicon tandem modules, PV is poised to surpass all other electricity generation technologies combined by the end of the decade.

filteredOut.title: Wettest February since 2011 cuts irradiance across most of Australia
filteredOut.summary: In a new weekly update for pv magazine, Solcast, a DNV company, reports that Australia's wettest February in 15 years produced solar irradiance deficits of 15–30% across the country's central and eastern regions.

events.title: Form Energy signs 12GWh agreement to supply multi-day iron-air batteries to new US AI data centres
events.summary: Form Energy has signed a 12GWh supply agreement in the US for its iron-air batteries with AI data centre infrastructure developer Crusoe.

observed_facts: [Contract] Form Energy signs 12GWh agreement to supply multi-day iron-air batteries to new US AI data centres — Energy Storage News (2026-03-27)
insight.matched_pattern: Form Energy는 현재 1건의 이벤트가 집계됩니다. 패턴 확인에 충분한 신호 밀도가 아닙니다. 1차 리서치를 권장합니다.
may_indicate: 초기 단계 또는 공개 정보 제한적
주요 활동이 공개 소스에 아직 미반영 가능성
missing: 제3자 인증 없음 — 대부분의 규제 에너지 섹터에서 조달 전제 조건
insight.next_check: 직접 접촉 또는 채널 체크 권장
insight.note: 기존 이벤트에서만 생성됨. 수치/사실 생성 없음.
